name: Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop
name_original: Las Cárcavas - Pontón de la Oliva – circular por el río Lozoya
description: Mountain bike the moderate 17.2-mile Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop for reservoir views and charming black villages.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valdepeñas de la Sierra offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park. The region is characterized by dramatic geological formations like Las Cárcavas, deep river valleys carved by the Jarama River, and indigenous forests of oaks. Its varied geology, including slate and quartzite, creates a wide array of trails, from technical rocky paths to smoother forest tracks, providing a compelling landscape for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Valdepeñas De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Parra Weir – Pontón de la Oliva Dam loop from Pontón de la Oliva, a difficult 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route explores river views and limestone cliffs around the historic Pontón de la Oliva dam. Local mountain bikers also love the Bonaval Monastery Ruins – Roman Bridge of Valdesotos loop from Los Quintanares, a 32.4 miles (52.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Valdepeñas de la Sierra is defined by river canyons, unique geological formations like Las Cárcavas, and indigenous forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more demanding rides with significant elevation gain. The routes in Valdepeñas de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valdepeñas de la Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Valdepeñas de la Sierra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 23 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every mountain biker.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: The region is enjoyable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. During warmer months, the indigenous forests with robles and encinas provide welcome shade, making them suitable for rides. Winter mountain biking is also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: Yes, Valdepeñas de la Sierra offers 3 easy mountain bike routes. These trails are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 23 difficult routes available. One demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23749500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Parra Weir – Pontón de la Oliva Dam loop from Pontón de la Oliva&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.3-mile (48.8 km) path exploring river views and limestone cliffs. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17507961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonaval Monastery Ruins – Roman Bridge of Valdesotos loop from Los Quintanares&lt;/a&gt;, a 32.4-mile (52.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical sites.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Valdepeñas de la Sierra varies greatly depending on the route's distance and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004977865/senda-del-genaro-cerro-negro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.2 miles (27.7 km), typically takes around 2 hours 53 minutes. More challenging routes can take 4 hours or more.

Q: What unique natural features can I see while mountain biking in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: The region is rich in unique natural features. You can encounter the dramatic, reddish-hued geological formations known as &lt;strong&gt;Las Cárcavas&lt;/strong&gt;, sculpted by water erosion. The Jarama River carves impressive canyons and gorges, such as the Jarama Canyon near Valdesotos, offering scenic riverine environments. The diverse geology also means varied terrain, from technical rocky paths to smoother forest tracks through indigenous oak forests.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, several routes pass by notable landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a frequent starting point for routes exploring river views and limestone cliffs. You might also find routes connecting to the vicinity of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4606981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patones de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, a charming 'black architecture' village, or pass by the Bonaval Monastery Ruins and Roman Bridge of Valdesotos.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Valdepeñas de la Sierra are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004977865/senda-del-genaro-cerro-negro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23749500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Parra Weir – Pontón de la Oliva Dam loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: As part of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park, the area is home to diverse flora and fauna. While mountain biking, you might spot various bird species, and depending on the time of day and season, you could encounter local mammals. The lush indigenous forests provide a rich habitat for wildlife.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valdepeñas de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from technical rocky paths to smoother forest tracks, and the stunning natural features like the dramatic Las Cárcavas and the scenic river valleys.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer views of reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region features several reservoirs that can be viewed from certain routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a key feature for routes exploring river views and limestone cliffs. Additionally, highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6619443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are nearby, and some trails offer scenic viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where are common starting points or parking areas for mountain biking in Valdepeñas de la Sierra?
A: Common starting points for mountain bike routes often include the vicinity of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which is easily accessible. Villages like Valdepeñas de la Sierra itself or Los Quintanares also serve as convenient starting points with available parking, allowing access to various loops and trails.
tips: The La Parra dam was built with the purpose of relieving the Navarejos dam, which is located downstream of the Lozoya River. Due to more recent works, today the dam is in disuse but forms part of the well-known La Parra Dam - Pontón de la Oliva route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of well-surfaced dirt tracks and some asphalt sections, especially near villages. You'll also encounter slate paths that can be technically challenging, and for those seeking more adventure, there are variants with rocks and narrow paths. A full-suspension bike is recommended for this varied and potentially rugged surface.

faqs.question: How challenging is this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as moderate. While the 27.7 km distance can be completed in a few hours, the varied terrain, including technical slate sections and significant elevation changes, means it offers a good challenge. It's advisable to be prepared for some demanding segments.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to mountain bike the Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop are during spring and autumn. Temperatures are milder then, making for a more enjoyable ride. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop offers stunning views of the Atazar Reservoir. You'll also pass through areas with characteristic 'black architecture' villages. Keep an eye out for historical elements like abandoned corrals and huts. Two specific highlights on or near the route include La Parra Weir and the impressive Las Cárcavas - Pontón de la Oliva gorge.

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself doesn't explicitly prohibit dogs, it's a mountain biking route with varied terrain and potential encounters with wildlife. If you bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, can keep up with the pace, and are kept on a leash, especially in populated areas or near livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any fees to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop. It's a public trail in the Madrid region.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact loop are not provided, but the broader Senda del Genaro often uses villages like Patones de Arriba as starting points, where parking facilities may be available. It's recommended to check local village parking options or designated trail parking areas near the start of your chosen segment.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Many areas along the Senda del Genaro are quite remote with scarce water sources. It's highly advisable to carry sufficient food and water for your entire ride, especially given the trail's length and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Does this loop intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the GR 300 - Senda del Genaro, Senderos del Jarama, and Senda 2: Remontando el Lozoya hasta la Presa de la Parra, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including technical slate paths and rocky sections, a full-suspension mountain bike is highly recommended for the Senda del Genaro - Cerro Negro Loop. This will provide comfort and better handling over the more rugged parts of the trail.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, the technical sections, slate paths, and significant elevation changes might be challenging for absolute beginners. Riders with some mountain biking experience will likely enjoy it more. If you're new to the sport, consider shorter, less technical routes in the area first.

waypoints.name: Las Cárcavas - Pontón de la Oliva
